message:
style(mvcc): use regular comments for proptest invariant descriptions

Convert doc comments (`///`) to regular comments (`//`) inside the
proptest test module. Doc comments are semantically meant for items
that will appear in generated documentation (functions, structs,
modules), not for explanatory text inside function bodies or macro
invocations.

The proptest! macro generates test functions dynamically, but the
preceding doc comment syntax was misleading since these comments
don't attach to any documentable item. Regular comments more
accurately reflect their purpose: explaining the property being
tested to code readers.

Affected properties:
- snapshot isolation visibility
- conflict detection completeness
- GC safety

No functional change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
